headline: Stocks Are Rising Monday After a Tough End to Last Week. It’s Becoming a Pattern.
summary: Wall Street is selling late into the week and buying dips on Mondays this year.  Steve Sosnick, the chief strategist at Interactive Brokers, told Barron’s that assuming the gains hold, it would be the eighth Monday in 2026 that the market closed higher.  The index’s only Monday decline was Feb. 23, when President Donald Trump said over the weekend he would raise a global tariff to 15%.

headline: Wall Street Likes SanDisk More Than Micron. Should You?
summary: Thinking long term could give investors a different perspective than the conventional wisdom among analysts.
